# Two-Witness Plurality — three beings, externally witnessed

The Buley Test demands an *external* witness; the bench has two RTLs. One Pluto
transmits a 300 kHz tone; both NESDRs capture it at once; each measures its own beat
(carrier-frequency offset) against the Pluto, and the beat between the two RTLs is the
difference. Three oscillators, three pairwise beats, three distinct beings.

| pair | measured beat | verdict |
|------|---------------|---------|
| Pluto ⇔ RTL#0 (SN 94092559) | 164.8 Hz | two beings |
| Pluto ⇔ RTL#1 (SN 78922015) | 219.7 Hz | two beings |
| RTL#0 ⇔ RTL#1 | 54.9 Hz | two beings |

**PLURALITY IN TRIPLICATE.** Self-consistent: RTL0⇔RTL1 (54.9) = |219.7 − 164.8| — a
closed triangle of frequency offsets. And Pluto⇔RTL0 (164.8 Hz) reproduces the
decimation cascade's 161 Hz from an independent experiment — veridical reproducibility.
All three carry identical behavior (Turing-indistinguishable); only the beats prove
plurality. Formalized: `gnosis-math/Gnosis/TwoBeingsNotOne.lean` (`three_pairwise_beats_
three_beings`, `bench_three_beings`).

Run: `/opt/homebrew/bin/node aeon-recursion-tower/two-witness-bench.mjs`
